diagrams.title: Basic Undirected Graph Incidence Matrix
diagrams.description: Shows 4-vertex graph with 4 edges forming a cycle, with corresponding incidence matrix table
elements: Matrix entries showing 1s where vertex incident to edge
Row labels on left, column labels on top
diagrams.purpose: Understanding basic incidence matrix structure and construction

diagrams.title: Directed Graph Incidence Matrix with Signs
diagrams.description: Same graph structure but with directed edges showing +1 and -1 entries
elements: +1 at head vertex, -1 at tail vertex
Color coding: green for +1, red for -1
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing sign convention for directed graphs and verifying column sum property

diagrams.title: Row Sum = Degree Visualization
diagrams.description: Shows how each row's sum equals vertex degree with visual edge counting
elements: Arrow from each incident edge to 1 in matrix
diagrams.purpose: Reinforcing property that row sum equals degree for quick verification

diagrams.title: Sparse vs Dense Matrix Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison showing why incidence matrix is efficient for sparse graphs
diagrams.purpose: Understanding space efficiency of incidence matrix for real-world applications

diagrams.title: Rank and Connectivity
diagrams.description: Shows connected and disconnected graphs to illustrate rank formula
diagrams.purpose: Visual proof and understanding of rank formula for connected vs disconnected graphs
